description: V0 bootstrap of the mifunedev/skills portable cross-agent skill library. Authors three seed skills (open-harness-review, docker-sandbox-debug, github-prd), a hand-written registry.json, a Bash install.sh, a validate.sh, README, and CI — all living under .worktrees/project/mifunedev/skills/ on the harness branch.

userStories.title: Scaffold repo skeleton at .worktrees/project/mifunedev/skills/
userStories.description: As an installer-author, I want the repo layout from 01-architecture.md to exist on disk so subsequent stories can drop files into known locations.
acceptanceCriteria: Directory .worktrees/project/mifunedev/skills/ exists with subdirectories: skills/, scripts/, template/, docs/, .github/ISSUE_TEMPLATE/, .github/workflows/
Top-level LICENSE file contains Apache-2.0 text
Top-level CHANGELOG.md exists with an empty ## [Unreleased] header (Keep a Changelog format)
Top-level .gitignore excludes node_modules/, dist/, .DS_Store, and any build artifacts
Top-level .github/PULL_REQUEST_TEMPLATE.md exists (uppercase, GitHub-recognized) with sections: Summary, Skill changes, Testing
Top-level .github/ISSUE_TEMPLATE/new-skill.md and .github/ISSUE_TEMPLATE/bug.md exist (templates may be minimal)
All paths are case-sensitive matches for 01-architecture.md § Target repo layout
userStories.notes: Foundational — every other story drops files under this path.

userStories.title: Scaffold .claude-plugin/ placeholder directory
userStories.description: As V1 implementer, I want the .claude-plugin/ directory present from V0 so the V1 generator has a known location to write marketplace.json without disrupting layout.
acceptanceCriteria: Directory .worktrees/project/mifunedev/skills/.claude-plugin/ exists
Contains a README.md (per context/rules/directory-readme.md) explaining the directory is reserved for V1's marketplace.json and is intentionally empty in V0
No marketplace.json file is written in V0

userStories.title: Author seed skill open-harness-review
userStories.description: As an Open Harness user, I want a portable version of harness-audit so I can run the same pre-merge review from any Agent-Skills-compliant client.
acceptanceCriteria: Folder .worktrees/project/mifunedev/skills/skills/open-harness-review/ exists with SKILL.md

SKILL.md frontmatter has all required fields per 01-architecture.md § Frontmatter rules: name, description (≤ 1024 chars, includes both what + when), license (Apache-2.0), metadata.mifune.version (0.1.0), metadata.mifune.category (open-harness), metadata.mifune.requires-tools (array)
Body is ≤ 500 lines (progressive disclosure)
No Claude-Code-specific keys at top level; any client-specific extensions are nested under metadata.mifune.claude-code.*
No $CLAUDE_SKILL_DIR references — use relative paths only
Source content adapted from .claude/skills/harness-audit/SKILL.md with HTML comment attribution at the top of the body
Scores CURRENT under this harness's /skill-lint (using the recipe documented in US-010 pre-flight)
.claude/skills/harness-audit/ is byte-unchanged (verified by git diff --exit-code in US-010)
userStories.notes: Parallelizable with US-004, US-005. Source attribution required; original is protected-path.

userStories.title: Author seed skill docker-sandbox-debug
userStories.description: As a sandbox operator, I want a portable skill for diagnosing common Docker sandbox failures so any agent can guide me through them.
acceptanceCriteria: Folder .worktrees/project/mifunedev/skills/skills/docker-sandbox-debug/ exists with SKILL.md
Frontmatter conforms to the same rules as US-003 with category: dev-workflow, requires-tools: ["docker"]
Body covers at minimum: container not starting, port collisions, volume mount issues, tmux session lookup, log retrieval via docker logs / tmux capture-pane
No platform-locked instructions (works on Linux + macOS; Windows callout if relevant)
No Claude-Code-specific top-level frontmatter keys
Scores CURRENT under /skill-lint (using the recipe documented in US-010 pre-flight)
userStories.notes: Parallelizable with US-003, US-005. No protected-path source.

userStories.title: Author seed skill github-prd
userStories.description: As any AI agent user, I want a portable version of the /prd skill so I can generate PRDs from outside Claude Code.
acceptanceCriteria: Folder .worktrees/project/mifunedev/skills/skills/github-prd/ exists with SKILL.md
Frontmatter conforms to the rules with category: dev-workflow, requires-tools: ["gh"]
Body adapted from .claude/skills/prd/SKILL.md with HTML comment attribution; sections preserved: clarifying questions, PRD structure, output rules
All Claude-Code-specific argument-hint / paths / hook references are either removed or moved to metadata.mifune.claude-code.*
No $CLAUDE_SKILL_DIR references
Scores CURRENT under /skill-lint (using the recipe documented in US-010 pre-flight)
.claude/skills/prd/ is byte-unchanged (verified by git diff --exit-code in US-010)
userStories.notes: Parallelizable with US-003, US-004. Source attribution required; original is protected-path.

userStories.title: Hand-write registry.json v1 (V0 seed)
userStories.description: As a CLI implementer (future V1), I want registry.json to describe the three seed skills so the eventual generator has a reference to match. V0 override: file is hand-written; generator + drift-parity CI deferred to V1.
acceptanceCriteria: File .worktrees/project/mifunedev/skills/registry.json exists
First key in the file is a _v0_note string explaining the hand-written status and pointing at the V1 generator deferral

Top-level fields present: $schema (commit-relative path: ./docs/schema/registry.v1.json — V0 ships the schema in-repo), name (mifunedev-skills), description, version (CalVer matching today's date), owner.name, owner.url
skills array has exactly 3 entries: open-harness-review, docker-sandbox-debug, github-prd

Each skill entry has all fields from 01-architecture.md § registry.json format: name, path, version, checksum (sha256 of folder, algorithm below), description, category, requires-tools, clients, license, added, updated
node -e "JSON.parse(require('fs').readFileSync('registry.json'))" exits 0
docs/checksum.md exists and documents the exact algorithm: find skills/<name> -type f -not -path '*/.*' | LC_ALL=C sort | xargs sha256sum | sha256sum | cut -d' ' -f1
docs/schema/registry.v1.json exists as a placeholder JSON Schema file (V0 may ship a minimal schema or a TODO schema); marked V0-seed in a top-of-file comment
userStories.notes: Depends on US-003/US-004/US-005 (needs actual skill folders for checksum computation) AND US-001 scaffold.

userStories.title: Author scripts/install.sh (Bash installer)
userStories.description: As a no-Node, no-Docker user, I want a Bash one-liner that pulls a skill from the registry to my project, so I can adopt the library without any toolchain.
acceptanceCriteria: File .worktrees/project/mifunedev/skills/scripts/install.sh exists and is chmod +x
First line is #!/usr/bin/env bash and second line is set -euo pipefail
Accepts CLI: install.sh install <skill-name> [--scope project|user] [--client agents|claude|harness]
Default scope is project; default client is agents (writes to .agents/skills/<name>/)

--client claude writes to .claude/skills/<name>/; --client harness writes to BOTH paths using documented staging+rename+rollback sequence; cross-filesystem invocations exit non-zero with ERR: install path on different filesystem from working tree

Open-harness safeguard: when --client is claude or harness, abort with exit code 3 if cwd or any walk-up parent contains BOTH .claude/protected-paths.txt AND context/SOUL.md (this is the open-harness repo)
Resolves git working tree root by walking up from cwd looking for .git; if absent and --scope project, exit code 4 with ERR: --scope project requires a git working tree
After git clone --depth 1 --branch main, captures commit_sha = git rev-parse HEAD and writes that SHA to the lock file's commit field (V0 mitigation for P6)
Copies only the requested skills/<name>/ folder; verifies SKILL.md exists before declaring success
Writes a lock entry to .mifune/skills.lock per 02-install-system.md § Lock file schema at the resolved git root
Re-running is idempotent: prints "already installed at <path> (commit <sha>)" and exits 0 without overwriting; idempotency keyed on (skill-name, scope, client, commit-sha)
On failure exits non-zero with single-line error to stderr; leaves no partial files
Has a --help flag that prints synopsis and exits 0
Symlink mode and --scope user|system not supported in V0; passing them errors with exit code 5
Lints clean under shellcheck -x scripts/install.sh

Negative-test script scripts/test-install.sh exercises the four error paths (harness-safeguard, no-git-root, cross-filesystem, symlink/user-scope) and the happy path; exits 0 only when all six produce documented exit codes
userStories.notes: Depends on US-002 (lock schema knowledge + skill list) and US-001 scripts/ dir.

userStories.title: Author scripts/validate.sh (CI gatekeeper)
userStories.description: As a CI maintainer, I want a single script that runs all skill validations so CI is a one-liner.
acceptanceCriteria: File .worktrees/project/mifunedev/skills/scripts/validate.sh exists, chmod +x, set -euo pipefail
Iterates every skills/*/SKILL.md and runs skills-ref validate against each (using the pinned invocation from US-010 pre-flight)
Verifies registry.json parses as JSON and lists every skill folder under skills/

Checksum integrity check: for every entry in registry.json[].skills, recomputes the folder checksum using the algorithm in docs/checksum.md and compares against the registry value; exits non-zero on drift with ERR: checksum drift in skills/<name>/ ...
Verifies no skill body exceeds 500 lines
Verifies no SKILL.md references $CLAUDE_SKILL_DIR

Verifies no Claude-Code-specific frontmatter keys at top level (deny-list: disable-model-invocation, user-invocable, paths, context, agent, argument-hint, arguments, hooks); deny-list rationale documented in docs/portability.md and at top of validate.sh
Exits non-zero with a clear summary listing every offending skill and rule
Lints clean under shellcheck -x scripts/validate.sh
Helper scripts/refresh-checksums.sh exists, recomputes all checksums in-place in registry.json, and is the V0 manual workflow for "skill file changed"; documented in docs/checksum.md
userStories.notes: Depends on US-002 (registry + checksum doc) and US-003/US-004/US-005 (skills to validate).

userStories.title: Author repo README.md
userStories.description: As a new contributor or first-time user, I want a README that explains what the library is, how to install a skill, and how to add a new skill in <10 minutes.
acceptanceCriteria: File .worktrees/project/mifunedev/skills/README.md exists

Sections present: One-line intent, Install (curl one-liner with the exact command), Skill catalog (table of the 3 V0 skills with descriptions), Add a skill (numbered ≤10-step procedure), Layout (self-contained description; do NOT link to harness-internal spec paths), License (Apache-2.0)
Install one-liner uses exact pattern: curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mifunedev/skills/main/scripts/install.sh | bash -s -- install <skill-name> --scope project
Includes a prominent NOTE block stating the GitHub raw URL becomes live after the manual one-time push to mifunedev/skills (which is out of V0 scope)
Includes a one-line explanation of the | bash -s -- pattern so first-time users understand how to pass arguments to a piped script
Add a skill steps are numbered and each ≤1 sentence; a person following them top-to-bottom can produce a passing skill folder + registry.json entry without consulting any other doc
No box-drawing tree (per context/rules/directory-readme.md)
Time-to-add target stated explicitly: "Adding a new skill should take <10 minutes"
userStories.notes: Depends on US-006 (install command) and US-002 (catalog). Parallelizable with US-009.

userStories.title: Author CI workflow .github/workflows/ci.yml
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I want every PR validated automatically so we never merge a broken skill.
acceptanceCriteria: File .worktrees/project/mifunedev/skills/.github/workflows/ci.yml exists
Triggers on pull_request and push to main
Runs on ubuntu-latest

Steps in order: checkout, install skills-ref (using the EXACT pinned invocation documented in US-010 pre-flight — no "per upstream docs" hand-waving), install shellcheck, run scripts/validate.sh, run shellcheck -x scripts/*.sh
Workflow is < 60 lines and uses pinned action versions (no @main or @master)
Passes actionlint clean (if available; otherwise YAML lint clean)
userStories.notes: Depends on US-007 (validate.sh) + US-010 pre-flight (skills-ref pin). Parallelizable with US-008.

userStories.title: V0 acceptance verification (incl. blocking pre-flight)
userStories.description: As the release captain, I want every V0 acceptance gate from 06-roadmap.md § V0 verified locally so we can declare V0 done before the harness PR merges. Includes blocking pre-flight that MUST complete before US-003/US-004/US-005/US-007/US-009 land their CURRENT/PASS claims.
acceptanceCriteria: PRE-FLIGHT 1: Resolve Q3 — determine the exact invocation that runs this harness's /skill-lint against a skill folder at an arbitrary path. Document the recipe in tasks/mifune-skills-library/verification.md § skill-lint recipe.
PRE-FLIGHT 2: Resolve Q2 — determine the exact skills-ref install command + pin its version. Record the chosen invocation in verification.md § skills-ref invocation.

Fresh temp dir test: tmp=$(mktemp -d); cd "$tmp"; git init -q; bash <path-to-worktree>/scripts/install.sh install open-harness-review --scope project --client agents. Verify .agents/skills/open-harness-review/SKILL.md is written, .mifune/skills.lock has a 40-char commit SHA (not a branch name), script exits 0.
Idempotency: re-run the same command in the same $tmp. Verify prints "already installed at .agents/skills/open-harness-review (commit <sha>)", exits 0, and find output before/after matches.

Open-harness safeguard test: from /home/sandbox/harness, run bash .worktrees/project/mifunedev/skills/scripts/install.sh install open-harness-review --client harness. Verify exit code 3, documented ERR message, and .claude/skills/open-harness-review/ does NOT exist after the failed run.
No-git-root test: tmp2=$(mktemp -d); cd "$tmp2" (no git init); run install.sh install open-harness-review --scope project. Verify exit code 4.
Validation gate: run scripts/validate.sh; verify exits 0 with all three skills reported PASS and checksum integrity green.

Skill-lint gate: run /skill-lint against each of the three seed skill folders using the recipe documented in pre-flight 1; verify all three score CURRENT (record exact invocation + result in verification.md).
Source-skill integrity: run git diff --exit-code .claude/skills/harness-audit/ .claude/skills/prd/ from harness root; verify exits 0; record in verification.md.

Time-to-add: time the README's Add-a-skill procedure using a fresh test skill (hello-world); verify <10 minutes wall clock (record start + end timestamps). After timing, remove the test skill folder and revert any registry edit BEFORE final commit.
Write tasks/mifune-skills-library/verification.md with one section per gate, each containing the command run and observed output
userStories.notes: Pre-flight 1 + 2 are blocking dependencies for any other story claiming CURRENT/PASS. Final story; depends on all others for the verification ACs.
